creator: tijl@FreeBSD.org
text:
When linking a library libA with a library libB using libtool, if libB.la
exists, libtool will add all libraries libB.la refers to (dependency_libs
field) to the linker command line and store them in the dependency_libs
field of libA.la so everything that subsequently links with libA will also
link to these extra libraries.  This causes too much overlinking.

The attached patch modifies USES=libtool so it empties the dependency_libs
field in .la files during staging.  However, because .la files have very
limited use when dependency_libs is empty it makes sense to completely
remove them during staging.

So with this patch USES=libtool is modified to remove .la files and a new
form (USES=libtool:keepla) is introduced in case .la files need to be kept
(dependency_libs is still emptied).

The patch bumps PORTREVISION on all ports with USES=libtool that install
.la files.  Most ports are also changed to add :keepla because .la files
have to be kept around as long as there are dependent ports with .la files
that refer to them in their dependency_libs field.  In most cases :keepla
can be removed again as soon as all dependent ports that install .la
files have some form of USES=libtool added to their Makefile.
